skills.name: forge-execute
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I have a validation plan in e2e-evidence/validation-plan.md with 5 journeys. Running forge-execute now. First attempt on 'user-signup' fails: form submits but no confirmation page.
assertions: Directs user to read evidence from forge-attempt-1/user-signup/
Identifies root cause as frontend navigation logic (backend 200 OK but no redirect)
Prescribes fix + rebuild before re-execution
Re-executes into forge-attempt-2/user-signup/ and increments strike count in state

old_skill.key_miss: Analyze phase mentions 'sequential thinking to trace root cause' but does not explicitly instruct 'read the actual evidence from forge-attempt-N/ first'; classification list lacks concrete 'quote what you see' discipline.

with_skill.key_miss: None — new Phase 3 explicitly orders: (1) Read the actual evidence from forge-attempt-N/, (2) Compare to PASS criteria, (3) Classify cause (code bug/config/env/flaky), (4) Decide fixable or BLOCKED, with worked example on the exact 200-OK-but-no-redirect case.

skills.name: forge-team
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: Fullstack project: React + Node API + PostgreSQL. Running /forge-team. Platform detection finds all 3. Walk me through waves, parallel spawning, and dependency blocking.
assertions: Wave 0 DB spawned alone as foundation, waits for PASS
Wave 1 API spawned after DB PASS, waits for PASS
Wave 2 Web spawned after API PASS
FAIL propagates BLOCKED to dependents; lead writes final aggregated report

old_skill.key_miss: None — dependency graph, wave table, wave execution loop, BLOCKED handling, and final report template are all present.

with_skill.key_miss: None — same body as snapshot; new description adds load-order guidance (coordinated-validation vs forge-team) but core behavior unchanged for this eval.

skills.name: full-functional-audit
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: Running a full functional audit on a SaaS product before public launch. It has 40+ pages. How do I discover 'every feature' without spending 2 weeks? What's in scope?
assertions: Feature Inventory performed in Phase 2 before execution
Codebase scanned for routes/components (routing table, OpenAPI)
Features grouped by user journey; ~20 representative features targeted
Scope limited to user-facing features; documented in Executive Summary

old_skill.key_miss: Phase 2 names 'Feature Inventory' and mentions 'map every user-facing feature' + Executive Summary, but gives no guidance on codebase route/component scanning and no ~20-feature heuristic for large apps.

with_skill.key_miss: Same gaps — new version adds concrete evidence-capture commands (curl/simctl/Chrome DevTools) but does not add the route-scanning + journey-grouping + ~20-feature heuristic needed to answer 'how to scope a 40+ page SaaS audit'.

skills.name: functional-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I've built a React app with a Node.js backend that talks to Postgres. The build succeeds but I don't know if the end-to-end flow actually works.
assertions: Recommends bottom-up order: DB -> API -> Frontend
References the 4-step Build -> Exercise -> Capture -> Verdict protocol
Points to fullstack-validation or e2e-validate for multi-layer orchestration
No mention of jest/mocha/pytest/unit tests

old_skill.key_miss: None — iron rule bans test frameworks, 4-step protocol is explicit, bottom-up diagram present, platform table points to fullstack-validation.

with_skill.key_miss: None — adds richer load-next guidance ('load e2e-validate if orchestrating') and clearer 'validate/verify' trigger phrasing; all four assertions still met.

skills.name: gate-validation-discipline
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: My iOS team says they've tested the login flow: app builds, logs in, shows dashboard. They're ready to ship. I have 10 minutes to verify before merge.
assertions: Walks the 5-step Verification Loop: IDENTIFY -> LOCATE -> EXAMINE -> MATCH -> WRITE
Demands concrete artifacts: screenshots, logs, build output
Mandates read/view/quote of evidence (not trust of reports)
Flags 'we ran tests' / 'build succeeded' as insufficient without evidence

old_skill.key_miss: Verification Loop + checklist are present, but callout of specific false-PASS phrases ('build succeeded', 'tests pass') is not foregrounded — has to be inferred from general rules.

with_skill.key_miss: None — new 'Common failure modes' table explicitly names 'Exit code 0, must have worked', '200 OK on every endpoint', 'All 15 screenshots exist', 'Sub-agent reported PASS' as failure patterns with rebuttals, directly satisfying the flag-claims assertion.

skills.name: ios-simulator-control
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I need to validate an iOS app on iPhone 16 Pro simulator but I don't know if it's currently booted or which one to use.
assertions: Step 1: xcrun simctl list devices available
Step 2: xcrun simctl list devices booted to verify state
Step 3: xcrun simctl boot "iPhone 16 Pro" if not booted
Explains UDID usage for subsequent commands; verify, do not assume booted

old_skill.key_miss: None — Simulator Lifecycle section covers list-available, list-booted, boot-by-name, verify, and $UDID convention across App Operations.

with_skill.key_miss: None — body is identical; new description adds richer trigger phrases (simctl command, trigger deep link, record simulator video) but same content serves this eval.
